verses.text: The gatekeepers are appointed, among them Obed-edom, whose many sons came because 'God blessed him' — the man who once housed the ark. His earlier reverence bore lasting fruit. To welcome God's presence rightly opens the door to blessing for a household.

The gatekeepers are appointed, among them Obed-edom, whose many sons came because 'God blessed him' — the man who once housed the ark. His earlier reverence bore lasting fruit. To welcome God's presence rightly opens the door to blessing for a household.

The gatekeepers are appointed, among them Obed-edom, whose many sons came because 'God blessed him' — the man who once housed the ark. His earlier reverence bore lasting fruit. To welcome God's presence rightly opens the door to blessing for a household.

Obed-edom's sons and kinsmen are 'mighty men of valour,' able for the service. Guarding God's house was no menial task but an honoured trust. To keep the door of the LORD's house calls for strength and faithfulness.

Obed-edom's sons and kinsmen are 'mighty men of valour,' able for the service. Guarding God's house was no menial task but an honoured trust. To keep the door of the LORD's house calls for strength and faithfulness.

More gatekeeping families are named, set in their wards to minister in the house of the LORD. The doorkeepers had their dignity. Every station in God's service, even the gate, is an honoured ministry.

More gatekeeping families are named, set in their wards to minister in the house of the LORD. The doorkeepers had their dignity. Every station in God's service, even the gate, is an honoured ministry.

More gatekeeping families are named, set in their wards to minister in the house of the LORD. The doorkeepers had their dignity. Every station in God's service, even the gate, is an honoured ministry.

Lots are cast for the gates, 'as well the small as the great,' east, west, north, and south. Each keeper takes his post by God's appointment. Faithfulness at every gate guards the holy place, and none is favoured by rank.

Lots are cast for the gates, 'as well the small as the great,' east, west, north, and south. Each keeper takes his post by God's appointment. Faithfulness at every gate guards the holy place, and none is favoured by rank.
The number of guards at each gate is fixed, the wards carefully assigned. Order governs even the guarding of the doors. God is a God of order, and His house was kept with diligent, organized care.

Certain Levites are set over 'the treasures of the dedicated things,' the spoils devoted to maintain the house of the LORD. The sacred wealth is entrusted to faithful keepers. What is given to God must be managed with integrity and care.

Shelomith and his brethren oversee all the dedicated treasures, gifts from David and many captains 'out of the spoils won in battles.' The fruit of victory was consecrated to God. Faithful stewardship of holy things is itself a sacred ministry.

Other Levites serve as officers and judges 'for the outward business over Israel,' in all the matters of the LORD and the king. God's servants worked in governance as well as worship. There is no divide between the sacred and the just ordering of public life.
